headline: World War I
description: World War I (1914-1918) was the first industrial-scale global conflict. Trigger: assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand (June 28, 1914). Alliances: Central Powers (Germany, Austria-Hungary, Ottoman Empire) vs. Allies (France, UK, Russia, Italy, US from 1917). Trench warfare, machine guns, poison gas, tanks — ~20 million deaths.

citation.name: The Sleepwalkers: How Europe Went to War in 1914 (2025)
citation.author: Clark C.
